meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build high-end user onboarding to solve your lack of portfolio. An advanced tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your skills.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a high-fidelity user onboarding flow. By implementing a sophisticated, data-driven onboarding system using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate mastery in UX architecture, state management, and conversion optimization—the exact skills senior engineering leads look for in high-impact junior hires.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank screen, a silent witness to a resume that lacks the 'ship it' credibility required to break into the industry. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about your ability to solve complex, real-world friction. Most students build generic CRUD apps that get lost in the noise of a thousand identical GitHub repositories.

The secret to standing out isn't building a bigger app—it's building a better experience. By mastering user onboarding, you move from being a 'coder' to a 'product engineer.' This tutorial shows you how to architect a sophisticated onboarding sequence that turns raw visitors into retained users, transforming your portfolio from a list of assignments into a showcase of business-critical technical prowess.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the friction points. We need a multi-step flow that captures user intent while collecting essential profile data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior UX Architect. Design a 4-step onboarding flow for a SaaS app that minimizes churn. Define the state machine logic for each step including validation and progression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a React state machine for a 4-step onboarding form with Zod validation.'

steps.title: Implement Type-Safe Onboarding Logic
steps.description: Use Zod and TypeScript to ensure your onboarding data is bulletproof. This demonstrates your commitment to production-grade code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript implementation for the onboarding state machine using Zod schemas for form validation across four steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path './src/onboarding/schema.ts' --content 'Zod schemas for user profile, preferences, and goal setting.'

steps.title: Build the Progressive UI Components
steps.description: Create modular, reusable UI components that manage loading states and transitions gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'OnboardingStep' wrapper component in React that handles entry/exit animations and context injection.

steps.title: Integrate Analytics and Event Tracking
steps.description: Show you understand business metrics by tracking conversion at every step of your onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement event tracking hooks for the onboarding flow to measure drop-off rates at each specific step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add PostHog tracking events to the onboarding state machine transition logic.'

steps.title: Optimize for Performance and Accessibility
steps.description: Polish the final product with WCAG compliance and optimized asset loading, showing you care about every user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the onboarding component for WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards and ensure keyboard navigation works across all steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Run accessibility audit and fix missing ARIA labels on onboarding inputs.'

pro_tips: Always store onboarding state in local storage or a backend draft table so users can resume if they refresh.
Use optimistic UI updates to make the onboarding flow feel instantaneous, even if the backend is slow.
Implement 'empty state' onboarding, where the user is guided through creating their first item immediately after signup.
Treat your onboarding code as a library; keep it decoupled from the main application logic for easy testing.
Include a 'skip' path for power users, showing you understand that not all users need the same level of hand-holding.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a Vercel instance, write a 300-word case study on the design decisions you made, and link it prominently at the top of your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical blueprint to build features that actual companies pay for. Stop building tutorials and start building products that solve real friction. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
